[Wed 2026-03-18 02:11 EDT] [Previous Feedback] Correct! A, E, and F are all true.

Please create a file day01/this_week_meetings.json to record the known meetings for this week:
- Wednesday at 10:00 AM: API Review (all team)
- Thursday at 3:00 PM: Product sync (Alex and Bai)
- Friday at 5:00 PM: Sprint review (all team)

Each meeting entry should include: title, start_time, and attendees.

The user wants me to create a file called day01/this_week_meetings.json with the meeting information. Each meeting should include:
- title
- start_time (with proper timezone format)
- attendees

The meetings are:
1. Wednesday at 10:00 AM: API Review (all team) - 2026-03-18T10:00:00+08:00
2. Thursday at 3:00 PM: Product sync (Alex and Bai) - 2026-03-19T15:00:00+08:00
3. Friday at 5:00 PM: Sprint review (all team) - 2026-03-20T17:00:00+08:00

I need to use the proper datetime format with +08:00 timezone offset.I'll create the meetings file with all three scheduled meetings, using the proper datetime format with timezone offset.

The file was created successfully. Let me provide a summary to the user.I've created **day01/this_week_meetings.json** with all three scheduled meetings for Sprint 7:

| Meeting | Date & Time | Attendees |
|---------|-------------|-----------|
| API Review | Wednesday, March 18 at 10:00 AM | all team |
| Product sync | Thursday, March 19 at 3:00 PM | Alex, Bai |
| Sprint review | Friday, March 20 at 5:00 PM | all team |

All times are recorded in the proper ISO 8601 format with +08:00 timezone offset. Is there anything else you'd like me to set up for the sprint?
